source_paper_title: Lower Complexity Bounds of First-Order Methods for Affinely Constrained Composite Nonconvex Problems
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Liu_2025, title={Lower Complexity Bounds of First-Order Methods for Affinely Constrained Composite Nonconvex Problems}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0377}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0377}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Liu, Wei and Lin, Qihang and Xu, Yangyang}, year={2025}, month=jun }
context:
Section 4 (Conclusion and Open Questions), page 25.

First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?
title: Tight oracle complexity for first-order methods with affine constraints and proximal regularizer
background_latex:
Consider the affinely constrained composite optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\; F_0(x):= f_0(x)+g(x) \quad \text{s.t.}\quad Ax+b=0,
\]
where:
1) \(A\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times d\}\) and \(b\in\mathbb{R}^n\).
2) \(f_0:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) is (possibly nonconvex) differentiable with \(L_f\)-Lipschitz gradient, i.e., \(\|\nabla f_0(x)-\nabla f_0(x')\|\le L_f\|x-x'\|\) for all \(x,x'\).
3) \(g:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) is proper, lower semicontinuous, and convex (possibly nonsmooth) and has the compositional structure
\[
 g(x)=\bar g(\bar A x+\bar b),
\]
for some \(\bar A\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n\times d}\), \(\bar b\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\), and a proper lower semicontinuous convex \(\bar g:\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) such that \(\operatorname{relint}(\operatorname{dom}(\bar g))\neq\emptyset\) and \(\operatorname{dom}(\bar g)\) is not a singleton.

Define the proximal mapping of \(g\) by
\[
\operatorname{prox}_{\eta g}(x):=\arg\min_{x'\in\mathbb{R}^d}\Bigl\{ g(x')+\frac{1}{2\eta}\|x'-x\|^2\Bigr\},\quad \eta>0.
\]

A first-order method (FOM) in the paper's Algorithm Class 1 starts from \(x^{(0)}\in\mathbb{R}^d\) and, for each iteration \(t\ge 1\), generates \(x^{(t)}\) using linear-span operations over previously obtained quantities and the oracle outputs. The oracle available is
\[
\mathrm{ORACLE1}(x,z,\eta)\mapsto \bigl(\nabla f_0(x),\; Ax,\; A^\top z,\; \operatorname{prox}_{\eta g}(x)\bigr),
\]
for any \(x\in\mathbb{R}^d\), \(z\in\mathbb{R}^n\), \(\eta>0\).

Given a tolerance \(\varepsilon\ge 0\), a point \(x^*\) is called an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point if there exists \(\gamma\in\mathbb{R}^n\) such that
\[
\max\Bigl\{ \operatorname{dist}\bigl(0,\nabla f_0(x^*)+A^\top\gamma+\partial g(x^*)\bigr),\; \|Ax^*+b\|\Bigr\}\le \varepsilon,
\]
where \(\partial g(x^*)\) is the convex subdifferential and \(\operatorname{dist}(0,S):=\inf_{s\in S}\|s\|\).

Let \(\Delta F_0 := F_0(x^{(0)})-\inf_x F_0(x)\) denote the initial objective gap. Let the joint condition number of the stacked constraint/regularizer maps be
\[
\kappa([\bar A;A]) := \sqrt{\frac{\lambda_{\max}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}{\lambda^+_{\min}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}},
\]
where \(\lambda_{\max}(\cdot)\) is the largest eigenvalue and \(\lambda^+_{\min}(\cdot)\) is the smallest positive eigenvalue.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a first-order method in Algorithm Class 1 (i.e., using only \(\mathrm{ORACLE1}\) and linear-span operations as specified) whose worst-case oracle complexity for producing an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point of every instance in the above class is
\[
\widetilde O\bigl(\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2}\bigr)
\]
(up to at most logarithmic factors), matching the lower bound
\[
\Omega\bigl(\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2}\bigr)
\]
proved in the paper for Algorithm Class 1.
latex:

where:
1) \(A\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times d\}\) and \(b\in\mathbb{R}^n\).

\[

for some \(\bar A\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n\times d}\), \(\bar b\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\), and a proper lower semicontinuous convex \(\bar g:\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) such that \(\operatorname{relint}(\operatorname{dom}(\bar g))\neq\emptyset\) and \(\operatorname{dom}(\bar g)\) is not a singleton.

\[

\[
\mathrm{ORACLE1}(x,z,\eta)\mapsto \bigl(\nabla f_0(x),\; Ax,\; A^\top z,\; \operatorname{prox}_{\eta g}(x)\bigr),

\[

Determine whether there exists a first-order method in Algorithm Class 1 (i.e., using only \(\mathrm{ORACLE1}\) and linear-span operations as specified) whose worst-case oracle complexity for producing an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point of every instance in the above class is
\[

\]
(up to at most logarithmic factors), matching the lower bound
\[

\]
proved in the paper for Algorithm Class 1.
open_problem_quote: First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?
subject_classification: First-order oracle complexity in constrained nonconvex composite optimization
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for an algorithm achieving a worst-case upper bound (tightness), not for disproving a universal claim by a finite counterexample. Automated instance search is unlikely to certify nonexistence or tightness in this general oracle model.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One could evolve candidate algorithms within the allowed oracle/span model and empirically test performance on parameterized hard instances, but automated evaluation cannot certify worst-case complexity bounds or prove matching \(\widetilde O(\cdot)\) rates without substantial human-guided theory.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
